# Archived Proposals — QA / Smoke Test Inventory

> Extended from the original **Electron QA / Smoke Test Inventory** to cover **every** archived OpenSpec proposal.
> Electron/platform proposals (35) extracted by parallel `google/gemma-4-31b-it` workers; all remaining (539) by parallel `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` workers — one worker process per proposal.
> Source: `openspec/changes/archive/`. Purpose: reference list of QA/smoke coverage to find and build **missing** tests.

## Summary

- **Proposals covered:** 574 / 574
- **Test / verification items extracted:** 11864
- **Proposals with no QA/smoke tests:** 7
- **By type:** unit 5878 · integration 1662 · e2e 372 · smoke 445 · manual 2347 · ci 1160
- **Extraction source:** electron/gemma 35 · deepseek 539

Each bullet: `**[type]** what it verifies — `file path` — (status)`. `type` ∈ unit·integration·e2e·smoke·manual·ci. Status reflects the proposal's claim at archive time, not a live audit.

---

# 2026-03

## 2026-03-22-auto-shutdown

- **[unit]** Config auto-shutdown fields (`autoShutdown`, `shutdownIdleSeconds`) exist in interface, defaults, and `loadConfig()` — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `autoShutdown` defaults to `true` when missing from config — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `shutdownIdleSeconds` defaults to `300` when missing from config — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `autoShutdown: false` disables auto-shutdown regardless of connection state — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `shutdownIdleSeconds: 60` uses 60s idle timeout — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Config with all fields present returns those values with defaults for omitted fields — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Config with partial fields returns supplied values + defaults for rest — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Empty or missing config returns all default values — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Config with auto-shutdown fields (`autoShutdown: false`, `shutdownIdleSeconds: 60`) returns those values + defaults — `src/shared/config.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Pi Gateway emits `onEmpty` when `connections.size` hits 0 on `session_unregister` — `src/server/pi-gateway.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Pi Gateway emits `onEmpty` when `connections.size` hits 0 on heartbeat timeout delete — `src/server/pi-gateway.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Pi Gateway emits `onConnection` when a new `session_register` adds to connections — `src/server/pi-gateway.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Pi Gateway exposes `onEmpty` and `onConnection` as optional callbacks — `src/server/pi-gateway.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server starts idle countdown when last pi session disconnects cleanly (`session_unregister`) — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server starts idle countdown when last pi session disconnects dirty (heartbeat timeout clears connection) — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server calls `server.stop()` + `process.exit(0)` when idle countdown reaches zero with no new pi connections — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server cancels idle countdown when a new pi session connects during countdown — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server starts idle countdown immediately on start when no pi sessions ever connect — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Browser connections do not prevent shutdown (only pi extension WS connections count) — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Idle timer wired via `piGateway.onEmpty` / `piGateway.onConnection` — `src/server/server.ts` — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` kills running stale process and removes PID file — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` just removes PID file if process is not running — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `createTunnel()` returns the same promise when called concurrently (mutex) — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** `releaseShare()` returns false when `zrok release` fails (best-effort, non-throwing) — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `releaseShare()` returns false for empty token without invoking zrok — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** `scavengeOrphanZrokProcesses()` returns empty array on `ps` failure — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `scavengeOrphanZrokProcesses()` skips self (current process PID) — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `getTunnelStatus()` returns unavailable when binary not available — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `getTunnelStatus()` returns inactive when binary available but no tunnel — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` calls platform `killProcess` when a live stale PID exists — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel-cleanup.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` skips `killProcess` when PID is already dead but still removes PID file — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel-cleanup.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` no-ops when no PID file exists — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel-cleanup.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `cleanupStaleZrok()` does not invoke `process.kill` directly — `src/server/__tests__/tunnel-cleanup.test.ts` — (status: implemented)

## 2026-03-31-pwa-install-prompt

- **[unit]** useInstallPrompt hook: canInstall remains false when browser never fires beforeinstallprompt — `tasks.md` §1.2, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Browser does not support beforeinstallprompt" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** useInstallPrompt hook: isInstalled true when display-mode: standalone matches — `tasks.md` §1.2, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "App is installed and running standalone" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** useInstallPrompt hook: isInstalled false when running in browser tab — `tasks.md` §1.2, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "App is running in browser tab" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** useInstallPrompt hook: isIOS true on iOS Safari (non-standalone) — `tasks.md` §1.2, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Running on iOS Safari" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** useInstallPrompt hook: isIOS false on non-iOS browser — `tasks.md` §1.2, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Running on non-iOS browser" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallButton: renders with icon when canInstall is true, calls prompt() on click — `tasks.md` §2.3, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Install is available" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallButton: not rendered when canInstall is false and isIOS is false — `tasks.md` §2.3, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Install is not available" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallButton: not rendered when isInstalled is true — `tasks.md` §2.3, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "App is already installed" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallBanner: appears on mobile Chromium with install button calling prompt() — `tasks.md` §3.4, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Chromium mobile with install available" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallBanner: shows iOS "Tap Share → Add to Home Screen" guidance on iOS — `tasks.md` §3.4, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "iOS mobile" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallBanner: dismissal persisted in localStorage key `pwa-install-dismissed`, banner does not reappear — `tasks.md` §3.4, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Banner dismissal" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallBanner: not rendered when isInstalled is true — `tasks.md` §3.4, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "Already installed" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** InstallBanner: not rendered when canInstall is false and isIOS is false — `tasks.md` §3.4, `specs/pwa-install-prompt/spec.md` "No install capability and not iOS" — (status: implemented)

# 2026-04

## 2026-04-01-flow-dashboard-integration

- **[unit]** Event reducer processes all 10 flow event types and produces correct `FlowState` transitions — `tasks.md 10.1` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Flow command detection from commands list using exclusion heuristic — `tasks.md 10.2` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Initial state `createInitialState()` has `flowState === null` — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_started` creates `FlowState` with pre-populated agent entries and `blockedBy` dependencies — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_agent_started` updates agent status to `"running"` and stores config metadata (label, model, card role) — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_agent_complete` updates agent status to `"complete"` or `"error"` with tokens and duration — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_tool_call` and `flow_tool_result` append to agent's `toolHistory` and update `recentTools` (last 3) — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_assistant_text` and `flow_thinking_text` append to agent's `detailHistory` — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_loop_iteration` updates target agent's `loopIteration` and `loopMax` — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `flow_complete` updates `flowState.status` and stores `FlowResult` data — `specs/event-reducer/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Flow commands filtered correctly via exclusion heuristic (only `source: "extension"` commands not in excluded set are launchable) — `specs/flow-card-launcher/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Bridge flow event forwarding: `pi.events` flow events produce correct `event_forward` messages — `tasks.md 10.3` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** All 10 `pi.events` → `eventType` mappings produce correct forwarded messages (`flow:flow-started` → `flow_started`, etc.) — `specs/flow-event-bridge/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Bridge registers listeners for all 10 `flow:*` events at activation — `specs/bridge-extension/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Bridge continues normally without crash when `pi.events` not available (pi-flows not installed) — `specs/bridge-extension/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Abort `flow_control` message received by bridge emits `flow:abort` on `pi.events` — `specs/bridge-extension/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Toggle autonomous `flow_control` message received by bridge emits `flow:toggle-autonomous` on `pi.events` — `specs/bridge-extension/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `flow:*` events fired before `sessionReady` are NOT forwarded by bridge — `specs/flow-event-bridge/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Autonomous mode state (`autonomousMode: boolean`) included in `flow_started` forwarded events — `specs/flow-event-bridge/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Browser sends `flow_control` → server forwards abort to bridge extension — `specs/flow-browser-protocol/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Browser sends `flow_control` → server forwards `flow_control` to bridge for toggle — `specs/flow-browser-protocol/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `FlowControlExtensionMessage` is valid member of `ServerToExtensionMessage` union — `specs/shared-protocol/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `FlowControlBrowserMessage` is valid member of `BrowserToServerMessage` union — `specs/shared-protocol/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `event_forward` messages with `eventType` starting with `flow_` are stored in memory event store and broadcast as `EventMessage` to subscribed browsers — `specs/shared-protocol/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Flow events included in `EventReplayMessage` when browser subscribes and requests replay — `specs/flow-server-state/spec.md` — (status: implemented)

## 2026-04-22-optimize-openspec-poll-burst

- **[unit]** mtime-gated poll does NOT call `pollOpenSpecAsync` for a cwd whose `openspec/changes` mtime is unchanged; DOES call it when mtime advances — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** semaphore caps concurrent `pollOpenSpecAsync` invocations at configured value — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** jitter phase is deterministic per cwd and distinct across cwds with the same interval — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `refreshOpenSpec(cwd)` bypasses mtime gate even when cache is fresh — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `reconfigurePolling({ pollIntervalSeconds: 60 })` replaces the timer cadence without losing cached data — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** config-openspec defaults, clamping upper/lower, non-number coercion, unknown keys ignored, round-trip stability — `packages/shared/src/__tests__/config-openspec.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** semaphore caps concurrency, releases on reject, FIFO ordering, resize loosens and tightens correctly, zero-max throws early — `packages/shared/src/__tests__/semaphore.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** cache miss on first call → CLI runs; second call with unchanged mtime → zero CLI calls; mtime advances on one change → exactly one `status` CLI call; change removed from list → its cache entry is pruned — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** interval respects config; jitter produces distinct per-cwd offsets; semaphore caps simultaneous spawns; reconfig mid-stream does not drop cache — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** cached data, call refresh → CLI runs; spam refresh 20× concurrently → at most `maxConcurrentSpawns` in flight at once — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** openspec poll tick does NOT invoke `scanPiResources`; dedicated pi-resources tick does — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** new settings section renders; editing a field and saving PUTs the new config; reset-to-defaults works — `packages/client/src/__tests__/settings-panel.test.tsx` — (status: planned)
- **[integration]** start server with `OPENSPEC_FIXTURE_DIR` containing a 30-change directory; assert a second poll tick makes zero CLI invocations when no files changed — `<n/a>` — (status: planned)
- **[ci]** `npm test` — all new tests pass, zero regressions — `<n/a>` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** `openspec validate optimize-openspec-poll-burst --strict` — passes — `<n/a>` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** start server against a repo with 30+ active changes; observe `pgrep -c openspec` during a steady-state minute — should be 0 most of the time, with occasional single spawns (not bursts of 30+) — `<n/a>` — (status: planned)

## 2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture

- **[integration]** Plugin manifest is parsed from `package.json` (manifests are validated) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin manifest format` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Adjacent `dashboard-plugin.json` takes precedence over `package.json` field — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin manifest format, Scenario: Adjacent dashboard-plugin.json takes precedence` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Invalid manifest is rejected at load time with fatal error and plugin marked failed — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin manifest format, Scenario: Invalid manifest is rejected at load time` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Package without `pi-dashboard-plugin` manifest is skipped silently — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin discovery scans monorepo packages on startup, Scenario: Package without manifest is skipped silently` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Disabled plugin (`plugins.<id>.enabled = false`) is skipped everywhere — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin discovery scans monorepo packages on startup, Scenario: Disabled plugin is skipped` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Discovery order is deterministic (by `priority` then alphabetical id) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin discovery scans monorepo packages on startup, Scenario: Discovery is deterministic` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Plugin registers REST routes via `ctx.fastify.register()` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Server-side plugin entry registration, Scenario: Plugin registers REST routes` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Plugin `registerPlugin` throw/reject is caught, plugin marked failed, `/api/health` exposed, other plugins continue — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Server-side plugin entry registration, Scenario: Plugin registration throws` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Disabled plugin's server entry is not loaded (no side effects) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Server-side plugin entry registration, Scenario: Disabled plugin's server entry is not loaded` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Generated registry imports plugin client entry with static named import — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Client-side plugin entries are bundled by Vite, Scenario: Generated registry imports plugin clients` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Disabled plugins are still bundled but filtered by runtime registry — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Client-side plugin entries are bundled by Vite, Scenario: Disabled plugins still bundled but inert` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Vite HMR regenerates registry on manifest change — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Client-side plugin entries are bundled by Vite, Scenario: Hot reload regenerates on manifest change` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Plugin bridge entry auto-registers in `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` under `dashboard-<plugin-id>` key — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Bridge entries auto-register as pi extensions, Scenario: Plugin bridge appears in pi extensions` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Disabling plugin removes bridge entry from settings — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Bridge entries auto-register as pi extensions, Scenario: Disabling plugin removes bridge entry` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** User-owned `settings.json` entries are preserved (dashboard only touches `dashboard-<plugin-id>` prefix) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Bridge entries auto-register as pi extensions, Scenario: User-owned entries are preserved` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Plugin reads session state via `pluginContext.useSessionState(session.id)` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin context API for read-only state and action dispatch, Scenario: Plugin reads session state` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Plugin dispatches browser-to-server message via `pluginContext.send()` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin context API for read-only state and action dispatch, Scenario: Plugin dispatches a browser-to-server message` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** Plugin importing from internal dashboard path triggers lint failure — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin context API for read-only state and action dispatch, Scenario: Plugin imports from internal path triggers test failure` — (status: planned)
- **[unit]** Default values are applied from JSON Schema default — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin settings persist under plugins.<id>.* namespace, Scenario: Default values applied from schema` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Invalid plugin config write is rejected (promise rejects, on-disk unchanged, no broadcast) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin settings persist under plugins.<id>.* namespace, Scenario: Invalid write rejected` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Cross-plugin namespace write attempt returns HTTP 403 — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin settings persist under plugins.<id>.* namespace, Scenario: Cross-plugin namespace access denied` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/config/plugins/:id` accepts partial config, persists, returns 200 — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: REST endpoint for plugin config writes, Scenario: Plugin config write succeeds` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/config/plugins/:id` with unknown plugin id returns HTTP 404 — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: REST endpoint for plugin config writes, Scenario: Unknown plugin id rejected` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/config/plugins/:id` for disabled plugin returns HTTP 409 — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: REST endpoint for plugin config writes, Scenario: Disabled plugin write rejected` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** All subscribed browsers receive `plugin_config_update` broadcast on config write — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Reactive plugin config broadcast, Scenario: All clients receive the update` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `usePluginConfig<T>()` hook re-renders consumers on `plugin_config_update` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Reactive plugin config broadcast, Scenario: Hook re-renders on update` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Broadcast payload does not leak cross-plugin namespaces — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Reactive plugin config broadcast, Scenario: Cross-plugin config not exposed in broadcast` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `/api/health` includes `plugins[]` array with `id`, `enabled`, `loaded`, `error?`, `claims` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin loader exposes status via /api/health, Scenario: Healthy plugin` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Failed plugin appears in `/api/health` with `loaded: false` and error message — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin loader exposes status via /api/health, Scenario: Failed plugin` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Disabled plugin appears in `/api/health` with `enabled: false, loaded: false` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin loader exposes status via /api/health, Scenario: Disabled plugin` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server-side plugin load failure does not crash the dashboard (other plugins load, failed slots empty) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin failure does not crash the shell, Scenario: Server-side load failure` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Client-side plugin component throw is caught by error boundary, no white-screen — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-plugin-loader/spec.md §Requirement: Plugin failure does not crash the shell, Scenario: Client-side runtime failure` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Slot id is referenced via typed `SlotId` import (not free string) — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-shell-slots/spec.md §Requirement: Slot taxonomy is a frozen, named list, Scenario: Slot id is referenced via type import` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Adding a new slot is a minor version bump of `pi-dashboard-shared` — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-shell-slots/spec.md §Requirement: Slot taxonomy is a frozen, named list, Scenario: Adding a new slot is a minor version bump` — (status: planned)
- **[unit]** Removing a slot is a major version bump; claiming plugins fail to load — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-shell-slots/spec.md §Requirement: Slot taxonomy is a frozen, named list, Scenario: Removing a slot is a major version bump` — (status: planned)
- **[integration]** React-only slot rejects descriptor contribution with warning — `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-26-dashboard-plugin-architecture/specs/dashboard-shell-slots/spec.md §Requirement: Each slot accepts a payload tier, Scenario: React-only slot rejects descriptor` — (status: implemented)

## 2026-04-27-diagnose-empty-mdns-scan

- **[unit]** `parseHostInput` parsing — `packages/client/src/__tests__/parse-host-input.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- full URL with port returns `{host, port}`
- full URL with path (strips path)
- URL with no port uses defaultPort
- `host:port` format
- bare hostname uses defaultPort
- bracketed IPv6 `[::1]:8000`
- bracketed IPv6 without port `[::1]`
- whitespace trimming
- empty / whitespace-only input returns `null`
- bare (un-bracketed) IPv6 returns `null` (ambiguous)
- invalid port (non-numeric, out of range) returns `null`
- malformed URL (`http://` with no host) returns `null`
- **[unit]** Existing `known-servers-sections` behaviour — `known-servers-sections.test.ts` (6 cases) — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** Run the full client test suite (parse-host-input + known-servers-sections) — `npm test` or equivalent — (status: implemented)

## 2026-04-27-fix-openspec-mtime-gate-blind-spots

- **[unit]** Stale counter detected — when `tasks.md` is edited in place (no rename, no entry change), a subsequent gated poll re-invokes `openspec list` once and `openspec status --change <name>` once for the edited change — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** No spurious spawn on idle ticks — polling twice with zero filesystem changes produces zero `openspec list` and zero `openspec status` spawns on the second tick — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Archive refresh is cheap — seed cache for a directory with 5 changes, simulate one being archived (directory moved out of `<changes>/`), call `refreshOpenSpec(cwd)`, assert `openspec list` runs exactly once and `openspec status` runs at most once — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** In-place edit to `tasks.md` re-runs status — see "Stale counter" test above (items 1.1 and 1.5 cover the same gate) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** In-place edit to `proposal.md` or `design.md` re-runs status for that change only, no spawns for other unchanged changes — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Second gated poll with unchanged directory mtimes makes zero CLI calls (pre-existing regression fence) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Per-change cache hit on dir-mtime-only path fails pre-implementation (used to confirm the bug exists) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `refreshOpenSpec(cwd)` after gate fix does not spawn `openspec list`/`status` when no tracked artifact changed — covered by "archive refresh is cheap" and "no spurious spawn" tests — `packages/server/src/__tests__/directory-service.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Toggle a task via `/api/openspec/tasks/toggle` then assert the resulting `openspec_update` broadcast carries the new `completedTasks` value within one poll tick (regression fence against re-introducing force-mode masking the gate) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/openspec-tasks-routes.test.ts` — (status: planned)
- **[ci]** Run full vitest suite (`npm test`); 3338 tests pass / 9 skipped / 0 failures — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** Re-run tests from §1 after implementation, confirm all 7 mtime-gate tests pass — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** Run `openspec validate fix-openspec-mtime-gate-blind-spots --strict`, fix any issues — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Edit a `tasks.md` outside the dashboard (separate terminal), wait for the next poll tick, confirm the change-row counter updates without manual refresh — `n/a` — (status: planned, deferred)
- **[manual]** Click "Archive completed" in folder OpenSpec section on a folder with ≥5 active changes; confirm UI updates within ~1 s (was ~3–5 s) — `n/a` — (status: planned, deferred)

- **[manual]** Manual `Refresh` button still works on populated folders (no regression) — n/a — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Verify no existing consumer breaks if `pending` is absent from `OpenSpecData` (default-falsy semantics) — `packages/shared/src/types.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server emits `{ initialized: false, pending: true, changes: [] }` when openspec dir exists but slow-poll cache is empty — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server emits `{ initialized: false, pending: false, changes: [] }` when `openspec/changes/` directory does not exist — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Server omits `pending` field (or emits `false`) once slow poll completes and cache holds `initialized: true` — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: implemented)

- **[integration]** Warm restart without data change: no redundant `openspec_update` emitted for cwd whose data matches prior cache — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: implemented)

- **[integration]** Browser sends `openspec_refresh` with cwd: server immediately re-polls, bypasses change detection, still respects concurrency cap, broadcasts result — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: unknown)
- **[integration]** New directory becomes known (pinned or session registers): server immediately polls it, bypassing jitter and change detection for first poll — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: unknown)
- **[integration]** Poll at configurable interval: changing `DashboardConfig.openspec.pollIntervalSeconds` via `PUT /api/config` takes effect without restart — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: unknown)
- **[integration]** Deterministic per-cwd phase offset: each cwd gets stable jitter offset in `[0, jitterSeconds)` from hash of its cwd, same offset every tick — `specs/server-openspec-polling/spec.md` — (status: unknown)

- **[unit]** `normalizePath` canonicalizes Unix paths: trailing separator removed, root preserved — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `samePath` multi-drive invariants: different drive letters (`A:\x` vs `B:\x`) → false; same-path-different-case-drive → true; UNC vs drive-letter → false; case-sensitivity of drive letter vs path components — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `samePath` cross-platform equality: Windows case-insensitive match, Windows separator-insensitive match, Windows trailing-separator-insensitive match, Linux case-sensitive non-match, macOS case-insensitive match, different paths never match — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `parsePathInput` edge cases: bare drive letter `B:` → `{ parent: "B:\\", partial: "" }` (no cwd leak); drive-relative `B:Dev` → `{ parent: "B:\\", partial: "Dev" }`; UNC roots; multi-drive symmetry; Windows path with trailing sep; Windows path with partial last segment; drive root alone; mixed separators tolerated; Unix absolute path variants — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `normalizePath` preserves drive-letter case in output (case folding only happens in `samePath` at compare time) — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Platform injection testability: Linux tests on Windows host pass `platform: "linux"`, Windows tests on Linux host pass `platform: "win32"` — `<packages/shared/src/__tests__/platform-paths.test.ts>` — (status: implemented)

## 2026-06-05-fix-file-mention-search-ranking

- **[unit]** — Exact basename match ranks above prefix, prefix ranks above substring, substring ranks above path-only hit — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Among equal scores, shallower depth wins; among equal depth, shorter path wins; final order is alphabetical and deterministic — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Bare `@` (empty query) returns top-level entries first alphabetically, not deep files — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Slash-aware split: query `@x/db/co` filters to candidates under `x/db/` and ranks leaf `co` as a basename (e.g. `x/db/conn.ts` before `x/db/proto.co`); candidates outside `x/db/` excluded — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Bare directory query `@x/db` surfaces the directory `x/db/` and its contents — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Trailing-slash query `@x/db/` lists directory contents ordered by depth/alpha — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Deep first-subtree no longer starves sibling top-level matches (regression test for the DFS-budget bug): construct tree where >20 files live under `a/deep/...` and a target file at root; assert root file appears — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — Cap is applied AFTER ranking: with >50 matches, the 50 returned are the highest-ranked, and `results.length === 50` — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** — `IGNORE_DIRS` and `depth > 6` still prune entries — `packages/extension/src/__tests__/` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** — Full test suite run (`npm test`) with `grep -nE 'FAIL|✗'` confirming green — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[smoke]** — `@<common-basename>` returns name matches first (e.g. `@package` surfaces root `package.json`) — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[smoke]** — >4000-entry regression test verifying shallow-first collection under visit budget — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[smoke]** — Query with >50 hits returns exactly 50 best matches — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** — Bare `@` shows top-level entries first, not arbitrary deep files — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** — `openspec validate fix-file-mention-search-ranking --strict` passes — `n/a` — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** Atomic tmp+rename write preserves original on write failure — `packages/shared/src/platform/openspec.ts` (task 1.6) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `workflowSetSignature` is order-independent over sorted de-duped names — `packages/shared/src/platform/openspec.ts` (task 1.6) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `OPENSPEC_CONFIG_PROFILE` and `OPENSPEC_UPDATE` recipes build expected argv — `packages/shared/src/platform/openspec.ts` (task 1.6) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `getOpenSpecUpdateSignature` / `setOpenSpecUpdateSignature` round-trips and persists across store reloads — `packages/server/src/preferences-store.ts` (task 2.3) — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/openspec/config` with `profile: "core"` invokes the CLI preset (`openspec config profile core`) — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 3.5, spec scenario "Save core profile uses the CLI preset") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/openspec/config` with `profile: "expanded"` writes JSON with `profile: "expanded"` and the 11-workflow array, preserving existing `delivery`, `telemetry`, `featureFlags` — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 3.5, spec scenario "Save expanded profile writes JSON with profile 'expanded'") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/openspec/config` with `profile: "custom"` writes JSON with `profile: "custom"` and exactly the submitted workflow subset — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 3.5, spec scenario "Save custom profile writes the selected subset") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** After successful config write, the server 30s `configCache` is invalidated (busted) — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 3.5) — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** After successful config write, no project cwd files are created or modified; only `~/.config/openspec/config.json` changes — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 3.5, spec scenario "Save leaves project working trees clean") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Direct write uses tmp-file + `rename()` so a concurrent reader never sees a partially written file — `specs/openspec-profile-config/spec.md` scenario "Write is atomic" — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** When the temp-file write fails, the original `config.json` is unchanged and the endpoint returns `{ success: false, error }` — `specs/openspec-profile-config/spec.md` scenario "Write failure leaves the original intact" — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/openspec/update { cwd }` runs `openspec update` in that directory, records the workflow-set signature, and returns `{ success: true }` — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 4.5, spec scenario "Update a single project") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `POST /api/openspec/update { all: true }` runs `openspec update` in every known cwd (session cwds + pinned dirs) and returns a per-cwd result list — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 4.5, spec scenario "Update all known projects") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** In the `all` path, one cwd's failure does not abort the remaining cwds — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 4.5) — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `GET /api/openspec/update-status` returns `up-to-date` when stored signature equals current global workflow-set signature — `packages/server/src/routes/openspec-routes.ts` (task 4.5, spec scenario "Project matching current config is up-to-date") — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `GET /api/openspec/update-status` returns `needs-update` when stored signature differs from current global signature — `specs/openspec-profile-config/spec.md` scenario "Project lagging the current config needs update" — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `GET /api/openspec/update-status` returns `unknown` when no signature has been recorded for a cwd — `specs/openspec-profile-config/spec.md` scenario "Never-updated project is unknown" — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** `GET /api/openspec/update-status` excludes cwds where `openspec init` never ran (no `<cwd>/openspec/` directory), and `POST /api/openspec/update { all: true }` skips them — `specs/openspec-profile-config/spec.md` scenario "Non-initialized directories are excluded" — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `saveOpenSpecConfig(profile, workflows)` POSTs the correct body to `/api/openspec/config` — `packages/client/src/lib/openspec-config-api.ts` (task 5.4) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `runOpenSpecUpdate({ cwd })` and `runOpenSpecUpdate({ all: true })` POST the correct bodies — `packages/client/src/lib/openspec-config-api.ts` (task 5.4) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** After a successful save, `__resetOpenSpecConfigCache()` is invoked and the config refetches — `packages/client/src/lib/openspec-config-api.ts` (task 5.4) — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** `useMessageHandler` test: `event_replay{events:[],isLast:true}` (empty session) clears loading flag — `packages/client/src/hooks/__tests__/useMessageHandler.test.ts` (inferred) — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** `useMessageHandler` test: `session_updated{dataUnavailable:true}` clears loading flag — `packages/client/src/hooks/__tests__/useMessageHandler.test.ts` (inferred) — (status: implemented)

- **[manual]** Host (localhost): open same session → spinner is sub-perceptible / brief, content shows immediately, no regression — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Open genuinely empty/new session → "No messages yet" still shows (no stuck spinner) — `n/a` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Loading indicator during history transfer — user opens old/ended session, subscribe sent, messages empty → loading indicator shown, "No messages yet" NOT shown — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Content replaces the indicator on first batch — loading indicator showing, first non-empty `event_replay` reduces → loading flag cleared, message bubbles rendered — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Genuinely empty session shows the placeholder — only `event_replay{events:[],isLast:true}` received → loading flag cleared, "No messages yet" rendered — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Warm re-subscribe after reconnect — reconnect clears subscriptions, client re-subscribes, messages momentarily empty → loading indicator shows until replayed content arrives — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Data-unavailable clears the indicator — loading indicator showing, `session_updated{dataUnavailable:true}` received → loading flag cleared — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Safety-net timeout clears a stranded indicator — loading indicator showing, no content/terminal/failure arrives within safety-net window → loading flag cleared, indicator does not persist — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)
- **[manual]** Scenario: Old server without explicit markers still recovers — server does not send clean terminal `event_replay{isLast:true}`, content does not arrive → safety-net timeout clears loading flag without server change — `spec.md` — (status: implemented)

## 2026-06-17-add-ws-broadcast-load-harness

- **[unit]** Byte accounting on send (1000 + 2000 bytes = 3000 bufferedAmount) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/draining-ws.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Drain clamps at zero (500 buffered, 2000 drained → 0, never negative) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/draining-ws.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** FIFO time-to-flush: small frame behind large frame on same socket flushes later (head-of-line blocking model) — `packages/server/src/__tests__/draining-ws.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[unit]** Closed / non-OPEN socket is skipped by the real gateway's fanout — `packages/server/src/__tests__/draining-ws.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Scenario A: 1 focused session, no openspec traffic — focused-event `timeToFlush` less than baseline budget at FAST and SLOW drain rates — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Scenario B: 1 focused + N idle cwds each firing `openspec_update` — focused-event `timeToFlush` assertion plus `wastedBytes(focusedSocket) > 0` proving the cross-cwd leak — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Scenario C: Scenario B with large per-cwd payload via `makeOpenSpecPayload` — latency grows with payload size; peak `bufferedAmount` recorded — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Scenario D: cold-boot connect burst (`broadcastToAll` per known dir) — dropped-frame count from `MAX_WS_BUFFER` guard plus connect-time focused-snapshot latency — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Scenario E: Scenario B with poll interval 60 s → 10 s over a fixed virtual window — periodic latency-spike signature aligned to tick boundaries — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Backpressure guard (`MAX_WS_BUFFER`) observed through real gateway code — socket's `send` skipped when `bufferedAmount` exceeds threshold, counted as dropped frame — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Serialize-once preserved across multiple draining sockets — every draining socket receives a byte-identical frame from the real gateway's broadcast — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[integration]** Signature classifier: periodic openspec bursts produce PERIODIC verdict; no competing traffic produces FLAT verdict — `packages/server/src/__tests__/browser-gateway-load.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** `npm test` passes with new harness files producing zero failures — `<n/a>` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** Deterministic execution: identical `timeToFlush` and `wastedBytes` values across 3 repeated runs (no clock flakiness) — `<n/a>` — (status: implemented)
- **[ci]** `openspec validate add-ws-broadcast-load-harness` passes — `<n/a>` — (status: implemented)

- **[unit]** Phase-2 DOX enforcement hook (Job 2): enforcement OFF → no nudge; stale/missing row → exactly one nudge; updating the row clears the flag (no repeat); AGENTS.md edit clears flags + reindexes; treeless edit → nudge names `kb dox init` — `kb-extension/src/__tests__/reindex.test.ts` — (status: implemented)
